title: Temperature and Sampling — Why the Same Question Gives Different Answers

format_discipline.notes: No frontmatter. Sidecar reading.meta.json present and valid (word_count=1389, estimated_read_minutes=7, citations_used=[1,2,3], sections correct). All six required sections present in correct order.

length.notes: 1389 words at 200 wpm = 6.95 min → 7 min. Both within standard-profile budget.

citations.notes: [1] cited 4 times; [2] cited 3 times; [3] cited 3 times. Bibliography entries [1],[2],[3] all match inline markers.

no_forward_references.notes: All terms reused from prior topics (forward pass 3.4, LLM/hallucination 3.2, vocabulary/tokens/autoregressive 3.4, weights 3.5) are permitted without re-definition. All new terms introduced in 3.6 (logits, probability distribution over tokens, temperature, greedy decoding, top-k sampling, top-p/nucleus sampling, deterministic output, stochastic output) are defined on first use within this reading.

faithfulness.notes: All 10 sampled assertions trace to corpus sections 5.1–5.5 and §7. Illustrative probability values in temperature table (e.g. 88%/10%/2% at temp 0.2 vs corpus ~95%/~4%/~1%) are both presented as approximate examples — acceptable divergence. API range claims (OpenAI 0–2.0, Anthropic 0–1.0) are factually correct and sourced from cited resources.

beginner_readability.notes: All 8 new terms bolded and defined at first use. Roulette-wheel analogy precedes formal definition of probability distribution. Temperature 'dial' metaphor precedes formal description. Sentence lengths short-to-medium; active voice; addresses learner as 'you'. No academic hedging detected. No paragraph introduces 3+ new terms simultaneously.

formatting.notes: All paragraphs 2–4 sentences. Three sampling strategies rendered as numbered list (lines 49–53). 'How to set it in practice' rendered as bulleted list. Inline example table ('The sky is ___') appears immediately when probability distributions are introduced, not deferred. All term definitions use bold-term / plain-meaning shape.

assessment_references.notes: No assessment IDs, due weeks, or grade weights appear in the reading. Correctly avoids the defect present in gold example 3-4.

scope.notes: All material (logits, probability distributions, temperature, greedy/top-k/top-p, practical task matching, accuracy anti-pattern) is directly on the topic headline. No adjacent concepts (RLHF, RAG, KV cache, batching, agent architectures) taught or introduced. API parameter names in 'In Practice' are practical temperature-setting guidance, fully on-scope.

gold_example_comparison.profile_note: Gold example is deep-depth (~1900 words); this reading is standard-depth (1389 words). Structural moves match: terms bolded and defined at first use, overview leads with big picture, worked example walks one concrete scenario, multi-item sets as lists, no assessment reference.
